pattern: Once the first two pieces of the digit string are fixed, the entire Fibonacci-like sequence is forced, so trying every pair of opening pieces and extending greedily either reconstructs the whole string or proves no split exists.
hints: After you commit to the first two numbers, every later number is determined by adding the previous two.
So the search is only over where the first and second pieces end; the rest must match exactly or the attempt fails.

Enumerate the split points of the first two pieces (rejecting leading zeros and values beyond a 32-bit signed range), then repeatedly append the sum of the last two and check it is the next substring; succeed when you consume the whole string with at least three numbers.

edge_inputs.description: a valid split exists with a leading-zero single piece allowed
no valid Fibonacci split is possible
a leading zero on a multi-digit piece blocks all splits
the textbook Fibonacci string splits cleanly
too short to hold three pieces
digits that never satisfy the recurrence
all same digit admits no valid sum chain

stress.description: build a genuine Fibonacci sequence then concatenate its digits, so a split is guaranteed to exist
stress.note: guarantees a solvable instance; the optimal must find some valid split

stress.description: random short digit strings, usually unsolvable, to exercise the empty-output path
stress.note: most random strings have no Fibonacci split
